Can you introduce the situation of the company recently disclosing to multiple automakers the output of intelligent driving solutions?Lotus Robotics, a subsidiary of Lotus' intelligent driving business, has partnered with several leading automotive manufacturers to provide industry partners with intelligent driving technology and software solutions. The company has officially announced the cooperation with Lynk & Co., Ltd. and Remote, including providing high-level intelligent driving solutions such as high-speed NOA and urban NOA for the first pure electric flagship sedan Z10 under the Lynk & Co brand. It provides a comprehensive set of advanced intelligent driving solutions including ADAS, highway NOA, urban NOA and intelligent driving tool chain for remote, which is expected to be applied to a number of its commercial vehicles (such as heavy trucks, light trucks, buses, etc.) in the future. These collaborations mark key milestones in Lotus' successful export and commercialization of its leading technology capabilities. All domestic vehicles under the Lotus brand (including SUVs and sedans) can be equipped with hardware supporting L4 level intelligent driving capability to carry Lotus advanced end-to-end intelligent driving service solutions. As cumulative residential vehicle deliveries continue to climb, the potential for future paid software subscription revenue is increasing.

question: 介绍一下路特斯的Vision80计划和Win26计划
rewritten: Can you introduce Lotus Technology's Vision80 plan and Win26 plan?
answer:
Lotus Technology 的 Vision80 和 Win26 计划是其长期战略的重要组成部分。Vision80 是 Lotus 为实现品牌转型和可持续
发展而制定的长远计划。该计划的目标是在 2028 年，即公司成立 80 周年之际，将 Lotus 打造成一个全球领先的高性能电动汽车品牌。Vision80 强调
了公司在电动化、智能化和可持续性方面的努力。例如，Lotus 已经推出了 E-segment 的电池电动车（BEV），并计划在未来几年内推出更多 BEV 车型。
此外，Lotus 还致力于通过减少废物、采用可再生能源和可回收材料来实现碳中和目标，预计在 2038 年前达成这一目标。Win26 计划则是 Lotus 在短期
至中期内的具体执行策略。该计划的目标是在 2026 年实现正向的 EBITDA 和正向的运营现金流。为了达到这一目标，Lotus 正在优化内部流程和结构，实施全
面的成本控制措施，并调整产品计划以适应全球多元化市场的需求。Win26 计划还强调了持续的产品创新和技术升级，例如推出 Hyper Hybrid EV 技术和智
能驾驶业务的发展。此外，Lotus 通过提高运营效率、减少运营费用，已经连续四个季度实现了成本的优化。这些计划共同推动 Lotus 在竞争激烈的汽车市场中保持领
先地位，并实现长期可持续发展。
content_list: The Vision80 strategy is dedicated to reviving the Lotus brand globally by focusing on customer satisfaction and shareholder returns. Under this strategy, the Win26 Plan aims for positive operating cash flow and Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) by 2026. Furthermore, Vision80 targets a 4% segment market share in 2028, referring to the luxury car market with an MSRP over $80,000. The graph illustrates the projected growth in Lotus vehicle deliveries. From an average of approximately 1,200 units delivered between 2018 and 2022, the number increased to 6,970 units in 2023. The company forecasts deliveries to reach 12,000 units in 2024, ultimately aiming for 30,000 units by the Win26 target year, accompanied by positive operating cash flow.

The Lotus WIN26 Plan targets positive operating cash flow and EBITDA in 2026 to enhance profitability and potentially increase returns to investors. Key milestones for 2026 include a vehicle delivery volume of 30,000 units and a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. The plan also aims for positive operating cash flow (OCF) and EBITDA by 2026. A significant increase in vehicle deliveries is projected, with approximately 12,000 units anticipated in 2024 and 30,000 units by 2026, compared to 6,970 units in 2023. The WIN26 Plan is structured around four pillars to drive profitability. The first pillar focuses on revenue by increasing the blended average selling price (ASP) with more limited edition models and enhancing the take rate of high-ASP and bespoke models. The second pillar emphasizes boosting other high-margin business revenues through increased high-margin intellectual property (IP) licensing and paid software subscriptions. The third pillar targets cost of goods sold (COGS) by reducing the bill of materials (BOM) annually and lowering trade-related COGS.

Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to introduce two new models in the near future. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification. As a leader in the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) model years ahead of its competitors and plans to continue launching its other BEV models. As part of its sustainability commitment, Lotus Technology aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2038. Lotus Technology expects to continue focusing on sustainability leadership through waste reduction and the ongoing adoption of renewable energy and recyclable materials. In addition, Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

To help engage the next generation of Lotus colleagues, Lotus Technology leverages its unique global appeal and stimulates the ambitions of the next generation, especially in the creative science, technology, engineering, art, and mathematics subjects. Lotus Technology helps carry out educational outreach programs and works with communities outside the core business to provide support and stability within those environments. Lotus Technology also adopts and aligns its strategy to external frameworks including the UN Global Compact, including commitments to transparent, responsible, and ethical business management. Lotus Technology released its first annual ESG Report on October 25, 2023, which documented its ESG strategy and highlighted its 2022 ESG achievements as part of Lotus’s advancement into a fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. The strategy focuses on six key fields: Nature Positive, Climate Neutrality, Sustainable Supply Chain, Inclusion and Equality, Community Commitment, and Transparent Governance. Lotus Technology joined the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) in May 2023 and has set targets aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for each field, embedding actionable frameworks into its operations to achieve the global goals.

What is Lotus Technology's future guidance?Lotus Technology's annual delivery target is 12,000 units, with year-over-year growth exceeding 70%. Through the Win26 program, Lotus Technology aims to achieve positive cash flow and profitability by 2026.

answer: 从2024年1月到10月，Lotus Technology Inc. 的总交付量为8,631辆。
content_list: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

question: 前10个月欧洲市场和中国市场的销量贡献分别是多少？

content_list: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Robotics reported a revenue of $19 million, which represents a year-over-year growth of 217%. The revenue from other customers soared to $11 million, marking a 450% increase as the external customer base expanded. Additionally, the revenue from Lotus itself grew to $8 million, reflecting a 100% year-over-year growth. The recognized revenue mix for the first nine months of 2024 comprised 58% from other customers and 42% from Lotus. The contract value distribution by region for the same period revealed that 70% originated from China, while Europe and the rest of the world (ROW) accounted for the remaining 30%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

Why is the sales volume in China low?China is a new market for Lotus. In the first half of this year, China contributed 25% of the brand's deliveries, while historically, China contributed only about 5% of Lotus's sales. In the context of a sharp decline in sales of luxury brands in China during the first half of this year, Lotus still achieved double-digit sales growth in the Chinese market. China is an important market for Lotus. Lotus will continue to enhance brand awareness in China and strengthen services to increase sales. ⚫ The main reason for the low gross margin is the increase in the cost of the Emira sports car due to inflation, as well as seasonal changes in the product sales mix. ⚫ Lotus's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus has taken a series of measures to respond to market changes (see below).

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

answer:
Eletre 已经在多个市场开始销售，包括东南亚、海湾合作委员会地区（如阿联酋）、新西兰、印度和埃及。此外，Eletre 也在欧洲市场上开始交付，并且已经开始接
受澳大利亚、日本和韩国市场的生产订单。
content_list: Lotus Technology has started deliveries of the Eletre to new markets, including the Gulf Cooperation Council region, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, following deliveries in Southeast Asia. Production orders for the Eletre have begun for new markets such as Australia, Japan, and Korea. Lotus Technology also started deliveries of the Emeya in Europe and is taking reservations for the Emeya in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. The company managed to maintain an average selling price of over US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. Lotus Technology has assessed evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, and the company has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA and positive operating cash flow in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost control measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

How is the company's overseas expansion situation?As a legendary brand with a history of 76 years, Lotus has a high reputation in the global market. The company currently has over 200 sales outlets in key global locations. The company's global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries. The company's lifestyle vehicles (Eletre and Emeya) are gradually entering new markets. After delivery in Southeast Asia, the Eletre SUV has started delivery in markets such as the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Eletre SUV production orders have commenced in Australia, Japan, and South Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has started delivery in Europe and has been pre-ordered in the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia.

Lotus Technology is a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of heritage and proven leadership in racetrack and road cars, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. The first lifestyle production vehicle, Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt electric powertrain architecture (EPA). Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. The second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of the Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to the Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis.

Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Deliveries of Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026.

The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds.

Lotus Technology is an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on Nasdaq as a foreign private issuer. Nasdaq listing rules permit a foreign private issuer like Lotus Technology to follow the corporate governance practices of its home country. Certain corporate governance practices in the Cayman Islands, which is Lotus Technology's home country, may differ significantly from Nasdaq corporate governance listing standards applicable to domestic U.S. companies. Lotus Technology relies on the home country practice exemption available to foreign private issuers for the requirement under (i) Nasdaq Rule 5605(c)(2)(A) that each of the members of Lotus Technology's audit committee must be an independent director as defined under Nasdaq Rule 5605(a)(2) and (ii) Nasdaq Rule 5620 that each issuer must hold an annual meeting of shareholders no later than one year after the end of the issuer’s fiscal year-end. Lotus Technology may choose to follow additional home country practices in the future. Lotus Technology is a “controlled company” as defined under the Nasdaq corporate governance rules because Mr. Shufu Li owns more than 50% of Lotus Technology's total voting power as of the date of this prospectus.

路特斯科技与吉利汽车的关系：（1）吉利汽车（Geely Auto）和路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）属于同一实际控制人。吉利汽车（Geely Auto）约75%的股份由李书福最终实际控制。而路特斯科技的单一最大股东是Lotus AdvancedTechnology Limited Partnership，持有路特斯科技27.6%，也由李书福实际控制。（2）此外， Lotus Technology 与 Geely Holding 签署了多项协议，包括技术许可、制造 合作和供应框架等，以确保供应链相关⻛险得到有效控制，并加速产品开发。这些合作使 Lotus Technology 能够利⽤Geely Holding 的制造能⼒、采购和供应链的议价能⼒、资本投资和运营⽀ 持，从⽽加快其⻋辆的市场投放速度。

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. On February 22, 2024 (the “Closing Date”), Lotus Technology consummated its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) pursuant to a merger agreement dated January 31, 2023 (as amended and restated dated as of October 11, 2023, the “Merger Agreement”) by and among LCAA, Lotus Technology, a Cayman Islands exempted company, Lotus Temp Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 2”).

Lotus Technology is an exempted company limited by shares incorporated in 2021 under the laws of the Cayman Islands. Lotus Technology reports under the Exchange Act as a non-U.S. company with foreign private issuer status. Under Rule 405 of the Securities Act, the determination of foreign private issuer status is made annually on the last business day of an issuer’s most recently completed second fiscal quarter. For as long as Lotus Technology qualifies as a foreign private issuer, Lotus Technology will be exempt from certain provisions of the Exchange Act that are applicable to U.S. domestic public companies, including: ● the rules under the Exchange Act requiring Lotus Technology to file quarterly reports on Form 10-Q or current reports on Form 8-K with the SEC; ● the sections of the Exchange Act regulating the solicitation of proxies, consents, or authorizations in respect of a security registered under the Exchange Act; ● the sections of the Exchange Act requiring insiders to file public reports of their share ownership and trading activities and liability for insiders who profit from trades made in a short period of time;

Many members of Lotus Technology's management team will have limited experience managing a publicly traded company, interacting with public company investors, and complying with the increasingly complex laws pertaining to public companies. Lotus Technology's management team may not successfully or efficiently manage the transition to being a public company subject to significant regulatory oversight and reporting obligations under federal securities laws and regulations, as well as the continuous scrutiny of securities analysts and investors. The need to establish the corporate infrastructure demanded of a public company may divert management's attention from implementing its growth strategy, which could prevent Lotus Technology from improving its business, financial condition, and results of operations. Furthermore, Lotus Technology expects these rules and regulations to make it more difficult and more expensive to obtain director and officer liability insurance, and consequently, the company may be required to incur substantial costs to maintain the same or similar coverage. These additional obligations could have a material adverse effect on Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects.

The company's first lifestyle production vehicle, Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt EPA. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu. Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Technology received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology issued 47,995,443 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark licenses. The above Reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

content_list: Competition Lotus Technology faces competition from both traditional luxury automotive developers and an increasing number of newer companies focused on electric and other alternative fuel vehicles. Lotus Technology believes the primary competitive factors on which Lotus Technology competes with its peers include, but are not limited to: ● brand recognition, prestige, and heritage; ● design, styling, and luxury; ● technological innovation; ● driver experience; ● product quality and performance; ● product reliability and safety; ● battery range, efficiency, and charging speeds; ● customer service and customer experience, including access to charging options and availability and terms of aftersales services; ● product pricing; ● management team experience in bringing electric vehicles and other disruptive technologies to market; ● manufacturing efficiency; ● environmental impact and perception; ● the degree and sophistication of related vehicle software. Lotus Technology believes that the company is favorably positioned to compete on the basis of these factors. However, many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors have substantially greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than the company. These competitors may be able to deploy greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sales, marketing, and support of their products.

Additionally, many of these competitors also have greater name recognition, longer operating histories, larger sales forces, broader customer and industry relationships, and other tangible and intangible resources that exceed those of Lotus Technology. These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, the company's products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in Lotus Technology's competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, relationship with Geely Holding, and the benefits obtained during the vehicle development phase, along with its future-proof approach, provide a competitive edge and enable the formulation of a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that is believed to generate significant competitive advantages, allowing the company to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

The operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, utilizes technology to create real-time 3D content for passengers.

rewritten: Please summarize the content of the share transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024.
answer:
2024年9月27日，Lotus Technology Innovative Limited（以下简称“LTIL”）与Geely UK Limited（以下简称
“Geely”）签订了一份股份出售和转让协议（以下简称“Share Transfer Agreement”）。根据该协议，LTIL同意出售，Geely同意购买L
otus Tech Innovation Center GmbH（以下简称“LTIC”）的800,000股股份，交易金额为10,880,000欧元。LTIC是一
家在德国注册的公司，是LTIL的全资子公司。该交易在签署股份转让协议时同时完成。交易完成后，Geely将持有LTIC 80%的股份。此信息基于2024年10月0
1日前的知识。
content_list: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

question: 2024年9月27日签署的股权转让协议完成之后，LTIC的股权结构是怎样的？

根据2024年9月27日签署的股份出售和转让协议完成后的股权结构，Geely UK Limited将持有Lotus Tech Innovation Center
 GmbH (LTIC) 80%的股份，而Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL) 将持有剩余的20%股份。此信息基
于2024年10月01日前的知识。

Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform, referred to as the “Geely License,” with a consideration of US$288.9 million. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022.

Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Group was appointed as the exclusive global distributor to distribute certain models of vehicles and to provide after-sale services, as well as brand, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles distributed by Lotus Group since January 31, 2023, for a cash consideration of GBP18,055 (equivalent to US$22,296). The Group determined that the acquisition of distribution rights did not result in a change in the reporting entity and was accounted for prospectively from the date of the transfer. The acquisition of the distribution rights was accounted for as a transaction between entities under common control, with the asset recognized at the historical cost of the ultimate parent. The difference between the purchase price of US$22,296 and the historical cost of nil was recorded as a distribution to shareholders in additional paid-in capital.

Simultaneously with the closing of the IPO, the Sponsor purchased an aggregate of 5,000,000 Private Placement Warrants (the “Private Placement Warrants”) at a price of $1.50 per Private Placement Warrant, for an aggregate purchase price of $7,500,000, in a private placement. Simultaneously with the closing of the exercise of the overallotment option, the Company completed the sale of an additional 486,784 Private Placement Warrants to the Sponsor, at a purchase price of $1.50 per Private Placement Warrant, generating gross proceeds of $730,176. A portion of the proceeds from the sales of Private Placement Warrants was added to the proceeds from the IPO held in the Trust Account. The Private Placement Warrants (including the Class A ordinary shares issuable upon exercise of the Private Placement Warrants) will not be transferable, assignable, or saleable until 30 days after the completion of the initial Business Combination, and they will not be redeemable by the Company (except as described in Note 7) as long as they are held by the Sponsor or its permitted transferees. The Sponsor, or its permitted transferees, have the option to exercise the Private Placement Warrants on a cashless basis.

The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. Upon the listing of ECARX Holding Inc. on December 21, 2022, the note was automatically converted into Class A ordinary shares of ECARX Holding Inc. at a conversion price of US$9.50 per share. question: 介绍一下Kershaw Health Limited
rewritten: Can you provide an introduction to Kershaw Health Limited?
answer:
Kershaw Health Limited 是一家在英格兰和威尔士注册的私人有限公司。根据2024年11月7日与Lotus Technology Inc.签订
的债券认购协议，Kershaw Health Limited同意认购并购买Lotus Technology Inc.发行的2029年到期的高级债券，本金金额为5
亿美元。Kershaw Health Limited需在债券发行日期后的30天内支付全部认购价格。 以上信息基于2024年11月8日之前的资料。
content_list: THIS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), dated November 7, 2024, is entered into by and between (i) Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Company”), and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”). Each of the Company and the Subscriber is referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”
Bond Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited, Form of Global Certificate.

On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement (the “Bond Subscription Agreement”) with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”), pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology hereby agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029 (the “Bond”), with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. The Subscriber shall pay or cause to be paid the subscription price in the amount of US$500,000,000 to Lotus Technology within thirty (30) days after the issue date of the Bond. Pursuant to the Bond Subscription Agreement, prior to the purchase price of the Bonds being paid in full by the Subscriber, Lotus Technology may at its option repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a repurchase price equal to the aggregate principal amount of the Bond paid by the Subscriber to the repurchase date (if any).

This Bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement, dated as of [●], 2024 between Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Issuer”) with its principal place of business at No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Bondholder”). Solely between the Issuer and the Bondholder, the terms of the Bond include those stated in the Subscription Agreement. The Bond is subject to all such terms, and holders are referred to the Subscription Agreement for a statement of all such terms. To the extent permitted by applicable law, in the event of any inconsistency between the terms of this Bond and the terms of the Subscription Agreement, the terms of the Subscription Agreement will control. For the avoidance of doubt, the Agents will not be bound by or have notice of any of the terms and provisions of the Subscription Agreement.

Copies of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Form of Global Certificate are included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1 and Exhibit 10.2, respectively, and the foregoing description of the Bond Subscription Agreement and the Bond is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto.

question: 公司对全球豪华电动车市场持什么观点？对行业竞争怎么看？
rewritten: What is Lotus Technology's view on the global luxury electric vehicle market?What is Lotus Technology's perspective on industry competition?
answer:
Lotus Technology 预计全球豪华电动汽车市场将快速增长，从2021年到2031年的复合年增长率将达到35%，到2031年市场将达到近190万辆。然
而，目前市场供应不足，只有大约10款豪华电动汽车在售，而传统内燃机豪华车型则超过100款，消费者选择有限。作为早期进入者，Lotus Technology 通过
推出其首款全电动高性能SUV Eletre，领先于竞争对手，旨在填补市场空白并抓住先发优势。Eletre 提供三种不同版本：Eletre、Eletre S 和 
Eletre R，其中 Eletre R 最大功率可达905马力，0到100公里/小时加速仅需2.95秒。公司在行业竞争中面临的主要因素包括品牌认知、声望和历史
；设计、风格和豪华感；技术创新；驾驶体验；产品质量和性能；产品可靠性和安全性；电池续航、效率和充电速度；客户服务和体验，包括充电选项的可获得性和售后服务的可用性
和条款；产品定价；管理团队在将电动汽车和其他颠覆性技术推向市场的经验；制造效率；环境影响和认知；以及相关车辆软件的复杂程度。尽管如此，许多当前和潜在的竞争对手拥
有远超 Lotus Technology 的财务、技术、制造、营销和其他资源，这些竞争对手可能能够投入更多资源用于设计、开发、制造、分销、推广、销售和产品支持。
此外，许多竞争对手还拥有更高的品牌认知度、更长的经营历史、更大的销售团队、更广泛的客户和行业关系，以及其他有形和无形资源。Lotus Technology 预期
未来汽车行业竞争将加剧，特别是在替代燃料车辆需求增加和全球汽车行业整合的背景下。这些竞争因素可能会影响公司的业务、财务状况、经营成果和前景。以上信息基于2024
年9月19日之前的认知。
content_list: The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market, growing at a CAGR of 10% from 2021 to 2031, according to Oliver Wyman. Additionally, driven by regulatory tailwinds and increasing sustainability awareness, the total global battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is expected to grow rapidly at a CAGR of 24% from 2021 to 2031. Within the global BEV market, the luxury BEV segment is expected to outgrow the mass market BEV segment, growing at a CAGR of 35% and reaching a total market size of 1.9 million units by 2025. Lotus Technology is well positioned to capitalize on the growth momentum of the global luxury BEV segment by leveraging the following strategies. Invest in brand equity and fully transform the brand. Leveraging Lotus Technology's racing heritage and proven leadership, the company intends to further elevate the brand by continuing to deliver a portfolio of fully electric and high-performance vehicles that satisfy customers’ expectations and broaden the customer base, particularly high net worth individuals and tech-savvy younger generations who value the brand’s DNA. Scale up and expand geographical presence. Lotus Technology intends to deepen its penetration across all regions.

The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The global automotive market is highly competitive and has historically been associated with significant barriers to entry, including large capital requirements and investment costs for developing, designing, manufacturing, and distributing vehicles, long lead times to bring vehicles to market from the concept and design stage, the need for specialized design and development expertise, regulatory requirements, establishing a brand name and image, and the need to establish sales and service locations. Lotus Technology has strategically entered into the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, and the company faces a variety of added challenges that a traditional automobile manufacturer would not encounter, including additional costs of developing and producing an electric powertrain that has comparable performance to a traditional gasoline engine in terms of range and power, inexperience with servicing electric vehicles, regulations associated with the transport of batteries, and the need to establish or provide access to sufficient charging locations and unproven high-volume customer demand for fully electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the sustainable luxury BEV market segment to become even more competitive in the future as additional players enter this segment. The company competes with competitors all around the world.

Competition Lotus Technology faces competition from both traditional luxury automotive developers and an increasing number of newer companies focused on electric and other alternative fuel vehicles. Lotus Technology believes the primary competitive factors on which Lotus Technology competes with its peers include, but are not limited to: ● brand recognition, prestige, and heritage; ● design, styling, and luxury; ● technological innovation; ● driver experience; ● product quality and performance; ● product reliability and safety; ● battery range, efficiency, and charging speeds; ● customer service and customer experience, including access to charging options and availability and terms of aftersales services; ● product pricing; ● management team experience in bringing electric vehicles and other disruptive technologies to market; ● manufacturing efficiency; ● environmental impact and perception; ● the degree and sophistication of related vehicle software. Lotus Technology believes that the company is favorably positioned to compete on the basis of these factors. However, many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors have substantially greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than the company. These competitors may be able to deploy greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sales, marketing, and support of their products.

These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, its products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in these competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that it believes generates significant competitive advantages, allowing it to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

Many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors, particularly international competitors, have significantly greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than Lotus Technology does and may be able to devote greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sale, and support of their products. Lotus Technology expects competition in the automotive industry to intensify in the future in light of increased demand and regulatory push for alternative fuel vehicles, continuing globalization, and consolidation in the worldwide automotive industry. Factors affecting competition include, among others, local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality and performance, technological innovation, product design and styling, pricing, safety, and customer service. Increased competition may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and increased inventory, which may adversely affect Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, operating results, and prospects. The company's ability to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its market will be fundamental to its future success in existing and new markets and its market share. There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its markets.

These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, the company's products. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, relationship with Geely Holding, and the benefits obtained during the vehicle development phase, along with its future-proof approach, provide a competitive edge and enable the formulation of a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that is believed to generate significant competitive advantages, allowing the company to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

As a leader in the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment, Lotus Technology has launched its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors and plans to continue launching additional BEV models. Lotus Technology also aims to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. As part of its sustainability commitment, Lotus Technology aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2038, with the Lotus Global Smart Factory, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. Lotus Technology expects to continue focusing on sustainability leadership through waste reduction and the ongoing adoption of renewable energy and recyclable materials.

Lotus Technology is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance lifestyle electric vehicles and has set high quality and safety standards for and makes continuous improvements on its vehicles and technologies, including sensory hardware and ADAS software. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties to other luxury auto brands and providing ADAS software subscriptions to its customers. Continue to launch new models and upgrade existing models. The successful launches of new models are critical for Lotus Technology to continue capturing market share in the luxury BEV market and strengthening its leadership position. Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles over the next two years, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification.

The Lotus brand is closely associated with “customer engagement” and “community building,” and in order to provide customers with a luxurious experience, Lotus Technology has adopted a direct-to-consumer global sales and distribution strategy that focuses on establishing and developing direct relationships with customers, especially in selected regions including China, Europe, and the U.S., which represent some of the key areas driving fast growth in the global battery electric vehicle markets. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market and aims to establish a total of over 300 stores globally by 2025. question: 三款Eletre有什么区别
rewritten: What are the differences between the three versions of the Eletre?
answer:
Eletre 提供了三种不同的版本，分别是 Eletre、Eletre S 和 Eletre R，以满足不同客户的需求。1. **Eletre** 和 **El
etre S**：   - **动力系统**：这两款版本均采用 450 千瓦单速版本，配备 112 千瓦时电池包。   - **续航里程**：根据 WLTP 标
准，最大续航里程为 600 公里。   - **性能**：Eletre 和 Eletre S 可以在 4.5 秒内从 0 加速到 100 公里/小时，从 80 
到 120 公里/小时的加速时间不到 2.2 秒，最大扭矩为 710 牛米。   - **定位**：Eletre S 更注重提供更长的续航里程和更高的舒适性。2
. **Eletre R**：   - **动力系统**：Eletre R 采用 675 千瓦双速系统，同样配备 112 千瓦时电池包。   - **续航里程*
*：根据 WLTP 标准，最大续航里程为 490 公里。   - **性能**：Eletre R 可以在 2.95 秒内从 0 加速到 100 公里/小时，从 
80 到 120 公里/小时的加速时间不到 1.9 秒，最大扭矩为 985 牛米，最大马力为 905 马力。   - **定位**：Eletre R 针对追求速
度和操控性能的客户，提供额外的赛道模式。这些版本在性能、续航里程和配置上有所不同，以满足不同客户的需求。以上信息基于 2024 年 9 月 19 日前的知识。
content_list: The company also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis. The Eletre is the first of Lotus Technology's new breed of pure electric SUVs. It is the company's first lifestyle vehicle, which aims to set the standard for future lifestyle vehicles. The five-seater (four-seater as optional) measures 5,103 millimeters in length with a 3,019-millimeter wheelbase, providing customers with a spacious and comfortable riding experience. The Eletre comes with five standard drive modes, switchable by adjusting the front and rear wheel steering, damper settings, chassis control system, propulsion strategies, and accelerator pedal response. Three different versions of the Eletre are available, namely, the Eletre, the Eletre S, and the Eletre R, with the choice of two powertrains. These versions are designed to satisfy the various demands of customers — the Eletre R is tailored for customers who seek speed and control, while the Eletre S provides a longer range and more comfort. With an average MSRP higher than US$100,000, the Eletre and the Eletre S feature the 450 kilowatt single-speed version, with a maximum range of 600 kilometers (km) per full charge according to the Worldwide Harmonized Light Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP).

Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA, which is a newly debuted self-developed BEV platform initially based on the same foundation of Sustainable Experience Architecture, the world’s first open-source BEV architecture. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury OEMs. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Eletre in China and Europe in 2023, and planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of Emeya began in March 2024.

content_list: Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models currently in Lotus Technology's global sales and distribution network include Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, such as Evija (battery electric vehicle sports car) and Emira (internal combustion engine sports car).

Which remains an intrinsic part of the company's sports car DNA. In this instance, that innovative vehicle architecture was wrapped with a bold and sculpted design using cues clearly developed from the Evija. With the first customer cars scheduled for delivery in the spring of 2022, the biggest surprise on the Emira was that there would be a choice of two petrol engines from different manufacturers, both globally respected for their performance characteristics. The first 'Launch' cars would be available as limited-production 'First Edition' models powered by the much-loved and Lotus-honed 3.5-litre, Toyota-derived V6 engine, which had cut its Hethel teeth powering the Evora and Exige. But, from the summer of 2022, the first new Lotus sports car engine for more than a decade would make its debut, with the new Emira being powered by AMG.

The new-to-Lotus AMG unit was to be a significant addition. With the news that it would be the last new internal combustion engine-powered (ICE) vehicle to emerge from Hethel, on July 6th, 2021, Lotus unveiled the Lotus Emira. It featured all the hallmarks that the automotive world had come to expect from a Lotus: striking design, thrilling dynamic performance, best-in-class ride and handling, and outstanding aerodynamics.

Emira

The last new internal combustion engine-powered (ICE) vehicle to emerge from Hethel was unveiled by Lotus on July 6th, 2021. It featured all the hallmarks that the automotive world had come to expect from a Lotus: striking design, thrilling dynamic performance, best-in-class ride and handling, and outstanding aerodynamics. The Lotus Emira, Type 131, is powered by a turbocharged 2.0-litre four-cylinder 'i4' from the performance division of Mercedes-Benz. Working with Lotus for the first time, AMG came on board as a technical partner on the Emira program with some fundamental changes made to the in-line four-cylinder setup for its Lotus debut. Transverse-mounted and delivering rear-wheel drive, the unit was modified with a new air intake system and new exhaust to suit the character and mid-engined format of the Emira.

Following more than £100 million of new investment in the company's UK facilities, manufacturing of the Emira was set to take place at the home of Lotus in Hethel, Norfolk. The highly skilled operations teams were to be supported by new processes and technologies to enhance production efficiency, volume capability, and overall quality. Matt Windle added: "The Lotus Emira is the most accomplished Lotus road car ever to roll through the Hethel factory gates. Beautifully proportioned."

What is the product plan for the future? otus Technology currently has four models on sale: the pure electric hypercar EVIJA (priced above $2.2 million), the pure electric intelligent SUV ELETRE (priced above $100,000, E-class), the pure electric intelligent GT Sedan EMEYA (priced above $100,000, E-class), and the fuel-powered sports car EMIRA (priced above $85,000). Lotus Technology plans to launch a new model each in 2025 and 2026.

Lotus Technology has strategically entered into the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, and the company faces a variety of added challenges that a traditional automobile manufacturer would not encounter, including additional costs of developing and producing an electric powertrain that has comparable performance to a traditional gasoline engine in terms of range and power, inexperience with servicing electric vehicles, regulations associated with the transport of batteries, and the need to establish or provide access to sufficient charging locations and unproven high-volume customer demand for fully electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the sustainable luxury BEV market segment to become even more competitive in the future as additional players enter this segment. The company competes with competitors all around the world. Lotus Technology's vehicles also compete with internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles as well as new energy vehicles.

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. On February 22, 2024 (the “Closing Date”), Lotus Technology consummated its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) pursuant to a merger agreement dated January 31, 2023 (as amended and restated dated as of October 11, 2023, the “Merger Agreement”) by and among LCAA, Lotus Technology, a Cayman Islands exempted company, Lotus Temp Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 2”).

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

In connection with the Restructuring, the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and nominee shareholders of the former VIE entered into the VIE Restructuring Agreements in early 2023, pursuant to which, (i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd., each being a subsidiary of the former VIE; (ii) except for the ICP license, all of the former VIE’s assets and liabilities, including its business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees, were transferred to the WFOE or subsidiaries of the WFOE at nil consideration; and (iii) the VIE Agreements were terminated.

As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology's operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology does not have any VIE structure. Lotus Technology believes that the Restructuring did not and will not have any material impact on its operations and financial results. Lotus Technology faces various risks and uncertainties related to conducting business in mainland China. Lotus Technology's business operations are primarily conducted in mainland China, and the company is subject to complex and evolving laws and regulations in mainland China. For example, Lotus Technology faces risks associated with regulatory approvals on offshore offerings, antimonopoly regulatory actions, and oversight on cybersecurity and data privacy, which may impact the company's ability to conduct certain businesses, accept foreign investments, or list on a U.S. stock exchange. These risks could result in a material adverse change in Lotus Technology's operations and the value of its securities, significantly limit or completely hinder the company's ability to continue to offer securities to investors, or cause the value of such securities to significantly decline.

Prior to May 2023, LTC relied on contractual arrangements among Wuhan Lotus Technology Limited Company, or the WFOE, Wuhan Lotus E-commerce Co., Ltd., the former variable interest entity which is referred to as the “former VIE,” and its nominee shareholders to direct the business operations of the former VIE and its subsidiaries. Since early 2023, LTC has been implementing a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations, which is referred to as the “Restructuring.” In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the former VIE, and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements, or the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology's operations in mainland China are conducted by its subsidiaries, and it does not have any VIE structure. The holding company structure involves unique risks to investors. As a holding company, LTC may rely on dividends from its subsidiaries for cash requirements, including any payment of dividends to its shareholders.

During the year ended December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology has implemented the Restructuring to reorganize its organization and business operations. In connection with the Restructuring, the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which, (i) the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd., subsidiaries of the former VIE, in February 2023; (ii) all the former VIE’s assets and liabilities (excluding the ICP license), business contracts, intellectual properties, and employees were transferred to a subsidiary of the WFOE at nil consideration; and (iii) the VIE structure was terminated on June 30, 2023. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology Capital’s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology Capital does not have any VIE structure. Lotus Technology Capital believes that the Restructuring did not and will not have any material impact on its operations and financial results.

(“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

In 2023 and the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 and 4,873 vehicles, respectively, consisting of lifestyle vehicles and Lotus-brand sports cars that were distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology had 2,989 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 54.6% belong to the R&D division. The results of operations are affected by the following company-specific factors. The results of operations depend significantly on Lotus Technology's ability to achieve vehicle delivery targets, which impacts vehicle sales revenue. It is critical for Lotus Technology to successfully manage production ramp-up and quality control, in cooperation with Geely Holding, to deliver vehicles to customers in targeted volume and of high quality. Currently, Lotus Technology works with a BEV manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered 7,617 vehicles, which marks a 136% increase from the 3,221 units delivered during the same period in 2023. The cost of revenues also rose significantly by 109%, totaling $594 million compared to $284 million the previous year. As a result, gross profit was $59 million, up 75% from $34 million in 2023. However, the gross profit margin decreased to 9% from 11% in the previous year. For the third quarter of 2024, 2,744 vehicles were delivered, a 54% increase from 1,782 in the same quarter of 2023. Revenue in Q3 2024 was $255 million, a 36% increase over $188 million in Q3 2023. The cost of revenues for this quarter was $247 million, up 54% compared to $160 million in Q3 2023.

Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

The cost of revenues also rose significantly by 109%, totaling $594 million compared to $284 million the previous year. As a result, gross profit was $59 million, up 75% from $34 million in 2023. However, the gross profit margin decreased to 9% from 11% in the previous year. For the third quarter of 2024, 2,744 vehicles were delivered, a 54% increase from 1,782 in the same quarter of 2023. Revenue in Q3 2024 was $255 million, a 36% increase over $188 million in Q3 2023. The cost of revenues for this quarter was $247 million, up 54% compared to $160 million in Q3 2023.

How is the company's overseas expansion situation?As a legendary brand with a history of 76 years, Lotus has a high reputation in the global market. The company currently has over 200 sales outlets in key global locations. The company's global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries. The company's lifestyle vehicles (Eletre and Emeya) are gradually entering new markets. After delivery in Southeast Asia, the Eletre SUV has started delivery in markets such as the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Eletre SUV production orders have commenced in Australia, Japan, and South Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has started delivery in Europe and has been pre-ordered in the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia.

Powered by Lotus Technology's high-power dual motor, Emeya can deliver an acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in 2.8 seconds, making it one of the fastest electric GTs in the world. Equipped with Lotus Technology's supercharging feature, Emeya can reach a range of 150 km with five minutes of charging time and boost up to 80% capacity within 18 minutes of charging time using a 350 kW direct current fast charger. In addition, Emeya has been meticulously designed with the latest sustainable materials to reduce the carbon footprint of its production. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of Emeya in China in March 2024 and expects to commence deliveries in Europe in the second half of 2024. The average MSRP of Emeya exceeds US$100,000.
