question: 路特斯科技是否拥有路特斯品牌？
rewritten: Does Lotus Technology own the Lotus brand?
answer:
Lotus Tech 通过与 Group Lotus Limited 签订的商标许可协议，获得了“Lotus”商标的使用权，主要用于与生活方式车辆相关的业务（不
包括跑车）。根据协议，Lotus Tech 发行了 47,995,443 股普通股作为商标许可的对价。此外，Lotus Tech 通过其全资子公司和可变利益实体
（VIE）及其子公司，主要负责设计、开发和销售豪华电池电动汽车（BEV生活方式车型）和豪华跑车。因此，Lotus Tech 在很大程度上控制了 Lotus 品牌
的使用和推广。以上信息基于2024年9月19日之前的知识。
content_list: On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”), Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of Lotus Technology Inc. Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, Lotus Technology Inc. received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology Inc. conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively on lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those lifestyle vehicles that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for Lotus Technology Inc.’s business of providing related after-sale services for the lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively on related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars) such as accessories that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, produces, distributes, and sells or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions in relation to the business conducted by Lotus Technology Inc. as duly approved by Lotus Technology Inc.’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars).

On November 4, 2021, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of the Company, entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”). Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, the Group received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as the Group conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively for lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those vehicles that the Group designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells, or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for the Group’s business of providing related after-sale services for lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively for related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars), such as accessories that the Group designs, produces, distributes, and sells, or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions related to the business conducted by the Group as duly approved by the Group’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars). The Group intends to market and distribute its products under the “Lotus” brand indefinitely, and the trademark licenses are expected to contribute to cash flows indefinitely.

The equity interests of Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH were transferred to Lotus Technology Corporation on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited, or LGIL, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation received the “Lotus” trademark licenses as long as it conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology Corporation issued 216,700,000 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark. The above reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022. On November 12, 2021, the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and a third party established Ningbo Robotics Co., Ltd., or Ningbo Robotics, in which the former VIE held a 60% equity interest. In March 2022, the former VIE transferred its 60% legal equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to its then wholly-owned subsidiary, Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. On March 15, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) declared a 10-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend, which was distributed to all the shareholders of LTC in proportion to their respective shareholdings in LTC.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022.

Lotus Technology believes that its R&D capability is one of its key competitive strengths. Drawn from Lotus brand sports car design heritage, deep automotive expertise, and next-generation technologies, Lotus Technology's proprietary 800-volt EPA is a high-performance platform for luxury electric vehicles, which was developed over five years of R&D efforts. The platform features supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Such superior design enables Lotus Technology to quickly roll out new models and ramp up production with competitive performance attributes and achieve economies of scale. Aside from the EPA, Lotus Technology has developed a leading ADAS with fully embedded L4-ready hardware capabilities enabled by the world’s first deployable LiDAR system and proprietary software system. Lotus Technology's wholly-owned R&D facilities across Europe and China demonstrate a seamless collaboration among highly experienced and dedicated Lotus teams to support its world-class R&D capabilities. Lotus Technology manufactures all battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models through a contract manufacturing partnership with Geely Holding, utilizing Geely Holding’s newly constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated to electric vehicles in Wuhan, China.

Lotus Technology views the manufacturers and suppliers it collaborates with as key partners throughout the vehicle development process. Lotus Technology aims to leverage its partners’ industry expertise to ensure that each vehicle produced meets the company's strict quality standards. Lotus Technology's Collaboration with Geely Holding Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand.

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. On February 22, 2024 (the “Closing Date”), Lotus Technology consummated its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) pursuant to a merger agreement dated January 31, 2023 (as amended and restated dated as of October 11, 2023, the “Merger Agreement”) by and among LCAA, Lotus Technology, a Cayman Islands exempted company, Lotus Temp Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 1”), and Lotus EV Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (“Merger Sub 2”).

On November 11, 2021, Lotus Technology issued 95,990,886 ordinary shares to Lotus Technology International Investment Limited, which is ultimately 100% owned by Geely Holding. Through a series of reorganization steps (the “Reorganization”), including transferring the assets and employees in the Lotus BEV business unit of Ningbo Geely R&D into Wuhan Lotus Cars and transferring the equity of Wuhan Lotus Cars to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology gained control over the WFOE through Lotus HK on December 15, 2021. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu. Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Technology received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology issued 47,995,443 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark licenses. The above Reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

question: 路特斯科技的盈利水平和现金流情况怎么样？
rewritten: What is the profit level and cash flow situation of Lotus Technology?
answer:
Lotus Technology 自成立以来尚未实现盈利。根据最新的财务数据，截至2024年6月30日，公司净亏损为4.603亿美元，2023年、2022年和2
021年的净亏损分别为7.503亿美元、7.246亿美元和1.105亿美元。此外，公司在2024年上半年的经营活动产生的净现金流出为4.684亿美元，2023年
、2022年和2021年的净现金流出分别为3.869亿美元、3.514亿美元和1.265亿美元。截至2024年6月30日，公司的累计赤字为20亿美元，而2023
年底、2022年底和2021年底的累计赤字分别为16亿美元、8.468亿美元和1.228亿美元。尽管目前的财务状况较为紧张，但Lotus Technology 
通过 Win26 计划，计划在2026年实现正现金流和盈利。公司还设定了每年交付12,000辆汽车的目标，年增长率超过70%。以上信息基于2024年10月16日
之前的知识。
content_list: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. The company incurred a net loss of US\$460.3 million, US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$468.4 million, US\$386.9 million, US\$351.4 million, and US\$126.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. As of June 30, 2024, and as of December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company's accumulated deficit was US\$2.0 billion, US\$1.6 billion, US\$846.8 million, and US\$122.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$49.5 million, US\$213.7 million, US\$133.3 million, and US\$34.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. The company will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations.

If Lotus Technology does not effectively manage its cash and other liquid financial assets, execute its plan to increase profitability, and obtain additional financing, it may not be able to continue as a going concern. For the three months ended September 30, 2024, Lotus reported revenue of $254.7 million, compared to $187.9 million in the same period in 2023. The cost of revenues was $246.5 million, resulting in a gross profit of $8.2 million, down from $27.6 million in the prior year. Operating expenses included research and development costs of $52.7 million and selling and marketing expenses of $55.5 million. General and administrative expenses amounted to $63.4 million. Government grants contributed $3.3 million, bringing total operating expenses to $168.2 million, a reduction from $191.1 million in 2023. The operating loss for the quarter was $160 million, slightly lower than the $163.5 million loss in the same period last year. Interest expenses increased to $8.8 million, while interest income rose to $6.6 million. Net investment income was $7.3 million. Foreign currency exchange gains were reported at $19.4 million, while changes in the fair value of liabilities contributed to additional financial impacts.

The Lotus WIN26 Plan targets positive operating cash flow and EBITDA in 2026 to enhance profitability and potentially increase returns to investors. Key milestones for 2026 include a vehicle delivery volume of 30,000 units and a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. The plan also aims for positive operating cash flow (OCF) and EBITDA by 2026. A significant increase in vehicle deliveries is projected, with approximately 12,000 units anticipated in 2024 and 30,000 units by 2026, compared to 6,970 units in 2023. The WIN26 Plan is structured around four pillars to drive profitability. The first pillar focuses on revenue by increasing the blended average selling price (ASP) with more limited edition models and enhancing the take rate of high-ASP and bespoke models. The second pillar emphasizes boosting other high-margin business revenues through increased high-margin intellectual property (IP) licensing and paid software subscriptions. The third pillar targets cost of goods sold (COGS) by reducing the bill of materials (BOM) annually and lowering trade-related COGS.

question: 公司如何对待用户的个人数据？
rewritten: How does Lotus Technology handle users' personal data?
answer:
Lotus Technology 采取了严格的措施来处理用户的个人数据。公司使用车辆的电子系统记录某些信息，以帮助进行车辆诊断、维修和维护，以及优化驾驶和乘坐体
 验。这些数据的收集是在必要权限下进行的。为了确保数据安全，Lotus Technology 采用了先进的加密技术和其他安全措施，以防止数据丢失和其他安全漏洞。
此 外，公司还制定了严格的信息安全政策，以遵守各种司法管辖区的数据隐私和安全法律法规。 这些法律法规包括但不限于中国的网络安全法、民法典、数据安全法和个人信息保
护 法，以及欧盟的《通用数据保护条例》（GDPR）等。Lotus Technology 会密切监控这些法律法规的发展，并评估其对公司当前业务实践的潜在影响。 L
otus Technology 也意识到，任何未经授权访问车辆、系统或数据的报告，无论其真实性如何，都可能对公司的品牌和业务产生负面影响。因此，公司致力于确保数
据 的安全性和合规性，以保护用户的信息和公司的业务。 以上信息基于2024年9月19日之前的知识。

Lotus Technology retains certain information about its customers, which may subject the company to complex and evolving laws and regulations regarding cybersecurity, privacy, data protection, and information security in various jurisdictions in which it operates. Lotus Technology uses its vehicles’ electronic systems to log, with necessary permission, certain information about each vehicle’s use in order to aid Lotus Technology in vehicle diagnostics, repair, and maintenance, as well as to help optimize the driving and riding experiences. Lotus Technology's customers may object to the use of this data, which may harm the company’s business. Lotus Technology has adopted strict information security policies and deployed advanced security measures to comply with these requirements and to prevent data loss and other security breaches, including, among others, advanced encryption technologies.

Furthermore, organizations and individuals must not illegally collect, use, process, or disseminate the personal information of others, nor illegally trade, provide, or disclose the personal information of others. According to the Provisions on Automobile Data, automobile data handlers (including automobile manufacturers, component and parts suppliers, software suppliers, dealers, maintenance organizations, and ride-hailing and sharing service enterprises) shall process automobile data (including personal information and important data during the design, production, sales, use, operation, and maintenance of vehicles) in a lawful, legitimate, specific, and clear manner. When processing personal information, automobile data handlers shall obtain personal consent or comply with other circumstances stipulated by laws and administrative regulations. If the automobile data handlers collect data from subjects outside the vehicle for the purpose of ensuring driving safety, but are unable to obtain consent from such subjects, the automobile data handlers shall anonymize the data by means such as deleting pictures containing identifiable natural persons or partially contouring the facial information in the pictures.

More narrow and specific federal laws apply to the processing or other use or treatment of certain types of personal data (including information related to health, credit, telecommunications, and telemarketing), or to the processing or use of personal data by certain types of entities (e.g., financial institutions). Additionally, the Federal Trade Commission may bring enforcement actions against companies that engage in the processing of personal data in a manner that constitutes an unfair or deceptive trade practice. Furthermore, the overwhelming majority of states have enacted laws related to data privacy. Perhaps the most stringent and comprehensive of those state laws is the California Consumer Privacy Act, as expanded and supplemented by the California Privacy Rights Act (effective January 1, 2023). To the extent state data privacy laws apply to Lotus Technology and its products or services, Lotus Technology aims to ensure compliance with the requirements of those laws.

If automobile data handlers collect data from individuals outside the vehicle for the purpose of ensuring driving safety, but are unable to obtain consent from such individuals, automobile data handlers shall anonymize the data by means such as deleting pictures containing identifiable natural persons or partially contouring the facial information in the pictures. According to the PRC Personal Information Protection Law adopted by the Standing Committee of the National People’s Congress on August 20, 2021, and implemented from November 1, 2021, the personal information of natural persons shall be protected by law. As the first systematic and comprehensive law specifically for the protection of personal information in the PRC, the Personal Information Protection Law provides, among others, that (i) an individual’s separate consent shall be obtained before the operation of such individual’s sensitive personal information, e.g., biometric characteristics and individual location tracking, (ii) personal information operators handling sensitive personal information shall notify individuals of the necessity of such operations and the influence on the individuals’ rights, (iii) if personal information operators reject individuals’ requests to exercise their rights, individuals may file a lawsuit with a People’s Court.

Lotus Technology prioritizes the trust of its customers and employees and places great emphasis on systems and product security, cybersecurity, and privacy. To protect its systems, products, and data, Lotus Technology applies a variety of technical and organizational security policies, procedures, technical controls, and protocols. Lotus Technology has a dedicated team of professionals that focuses on application, network, system, and product security based upon a clearly defined organizational operating model. Lotus Technology has obtained the ISO/IEC 27001 certifications (GB/T 22080-2016) and R155/R156 certifications. Lotus Technology has also commenced corporate-wide data privacy policies and controls with dedicated cross-functional resources. Lotus Technology implements enterprise vulnerability management processes that include periodic scans designed to identify security vulnerabilities and implement remediation. In addition, Lotus Technology conducts internal and external penetration tests, receives threat intelligence, follows incident response procedures, and remediates vulnerabilities according to severity and risk. Further, seeking to implement effective management, control, and protection, Lotus Technology has established a centralized, organization-wide view of information assets. Lotus Technology has instituted cybersecurity risk monitoring policies to detect threats and cybersecurity risks to its enterprise information assets and products.

Lotus Technology has implemented cybersecurity monitoring capabilities that collect and analyze telemetries from a wide range of sources and take proactive actions to ensure the security risk visualization of its systems and products. Lotus Technology's cloud security policies seek to enable secure cloud architecture deployments and extend security capabilities. Utilizing signed certificates, encryption keys, message authentication codes, and cryptographic algorithms, Lotus Technology adopts authentication and encryption to secure its products, software, vehicles and their components, and OTA updates. Additionally, Lotus Technology utilizes pre-condition checks, sequence and dependency execution, and failure recovery when performing updates during the OTA process. Lotus Technology's vehicle development involves a significant degree of automation and technology. With that level of complexity and interconnectivity in mind, Lotus Technology is building cybersecurity by the TARA process into its vehicle development process itself, with the intent of enabling the business to remain resilient to any potential attacks during vehicle development. The objective of Lotus Technology's privacy policies is to facilitate beneficial uses of data to improve its products and services while preserving customers’ privacy expectations and complying with applicable law. Global privacy laws and practices will guide the operational design, controls, procedures, and policies for Lotus Technology's program.

(iii) data handlers’ listing in Hong Kong that affects or may affect national security; (iv) other data processing activities that affect or may affect national security. Large internet platform operators who establish headquarters, operation centers, or research and development centers overseas shall report to the national cyberspace administration and the competent authorities. As of the date of this prospectus, the Regulations have not been formally adopted. Under the Several Provisions on Regulating the Market Order of Internet Information Services issued by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology on December 29, 2011, and effective on March 15, 2012, the Decision on Strengthening the Protection of Online Information issued by the Standing Committee of the National People’s Congress and implemented on December 28, 2012, the Order for the Protection of Telecommunications and Internet User Personal Information issued by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology and implemented on July 16, 2013, and the PRC Cybersecurity Law issued by the Standing Committee of the National People’s Congress on November 7, 2016, and implemented on June 1, 2017, any collection and use of a user’s personal information must be legal, rational, and necessary.

Additionally, they have enacted and implemented laws and regulations and proposed an additional regulatory agenda concerning data protection and privacy, under which internet service providers and other network operators are required to, among other things, clearly indicate the purposes, methods, and scope of any information collection and usage, obtain appropriate user consent, establish user information protection systems with appropriate remedial measures, and address national security concerns. For a comprehensive discussion on the aforementioned laws and regulations, see “Government Regulations    —      PRC Government Regulations   —   Regulations on Cyber Security and Privacy Protection.” Lotus Technology expects that its operations in the areas referenced above will receive greater public scrutiny and attention from regulators, as well as more frequent and rigorous investigations or reviews by regulators, which will increase compliance costs and subject the company to heightened risks. Lotus Technology is closely monitoring developments in the regulatory landscape and is constantly evaluating the potential impact of the PRC Cybersecurity Law, the Civil Code, the Data Security Law, the Personal Information Protection Law, and other laws and regulations on its current business practices. It also remains uncertain whether any future regulatory changes would impose additional restrictions on companies like Lotus Technology.

Under this scheme, entities operating information systems must conduct a thorough assessment of the risks and the conditions of their information and network systems to determine the level to which the entity’s information and network systems belong—from the lowest Level 1 to the highest Level 5—pursuant to a series of national standards on the grading and implementation of the classified protection of cybersecurity. The grading result will determine the set of security protection obligations that entities must comply with. Entities classified as Level 2 or above are required to report the grade to the government authority for examination and approval. On November 14, 2021, the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) released the Regulations on Network Data Security Management (draft for public comments), which provide that if a data processor that processes personal data of more than one million users intends to list in a foreign country, it must apply for a cybersecurity review. Pending the finalization, adoption, enforcement, and interpretation of these new measures and regulations, Lotus Technology cannot rule out the possibility that the measures and regulations may be enacted, interpreted, or implemented in ways that will negatively affect the company.

With that level of complexity and interconnectivity in mind, Lotus Technology is building cybersecurity through the TARA process into its vehicle development process itself, with the intent of enabling the business to remain resilient to any potential attacks during vehicle development. The objective of Lotus Technology's privacy policies is to facilitate beneficial uses of data to improve its products and services while preserving customers’ privacy expectations and complying with applicable law. Global privacy laws and practices will guide the operational design, controls, procedures, and policies for Lotus Technology's program. Lotus Technology's strategy accounts for increased risk as the business scales by addressing appropriate security and access controls for customer and employee information. A core tenet of Lotus Technology's privacy measures is to implement privacy-by-design principles in both software and hardware development throughout the organization. Lotus Technology's privacy measures will continue to evolve and adapt, utilizing best practices and tailored risk management frameworks, to allow for close collaboration across the organization, particularly between the information technology and legal functions, which is critical for effective privacy measures. Lotus Technology also works to increase cybersecurity and privacy awareness throughout the organization through education and training.

Regulations on Data Protection and Privacy EU Since the entry into force on May 25, 2018, of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), processing of personal data of individuals located in the European Economic Area (EEA), or conducted by any entity in the EEA, is subject to strict requirements centered around core principles and rights of such individuals to receive access to, rectify, or delete their personal data. The GDPR obliges Lotus Technology, where applicable, to ensure adherence to the principles of lawfulness, fairness, transparency, purpose limitation, data minimization, data accuracy, storage limitation, and integrity and confidentiality. The GDPR also requires Lotus Technology to mitigate potential data breaches and to, unless the data breach leads to a low risk for the rights and freedoms of data subjects, report data breaches to the data protection supervisory authority within 72 hours. On March 9, 2021, the European Data Protection Board adopted Guidelines 01/2020 on processing personal data in the context of connected vehicles and mobility-related applications, which stated that much of the data generated by a connected vehicle relates to a natural person who is identified or identifiable and thus constitutes personal data under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Lotus Technology's cloud security policies seek to enable secure cloud architecture deployments and extend security capabilities. Utilizing signed certificates, encryption keys, message authentication codes, and cryptographic algorithms, Lotus Technology has deployed authentication and encryption as part of its efforts to secure its products, software, vehicles and their components, and OTA updates. question: Kershaw Health Limited与Lotus Technology达成了什么协议？
rewritten: What agreement has Kershaw Health Limited reached with Lotus Technology?
answer:
Kershaw Health Limited 与 Lotus Technology Inc. 达成了一项债券认购协议（Bond Subscription Agr
eement），该协议于2024年11月7日签订。根据该协议，Kershaw Health Limited 同意认购并购买，而 Lotus Technology
 同意发行并出售一笔本金金额为5亿美元的高级债券（Bond），到期日为2029年。Kershaw Health Limited 应在债券发行日期后的30天内支付
或促使支付5亿美元的认购价格给 Lotus Technology。此外，根据债券认购协议，在Kershaw Health Limited 全额支付债券购买价格之
前，Lotus Technology 有权选择以等于Kershaw Health Limited 已支付的债券本金金额的回购价格，全部但不能部分地回购该债券。以
上信息基于2024年11月8日之前的知识。
content_list: Bond Subscription Agreement, dated November 7, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited, Form of Global Certificate.

THIS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT (this “Agreement”), dated November 7, 2024, is entered into by and between (i) Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Company”), and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”). Each of the Company and the Subscriber is referred to herein individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”

On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a bond subscription agreement (the “Bond Subscription Agreement”) with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Subscriber”), pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology hereby agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029 (the “Bond”), with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. The Subscriber shall pay or cause to be paid the subscription price in the amount of US$500,000,000 to Lotus Technology within thirty (30) days after the issue date of the Bond. Pursuant to the Bond Subscription Agreement, prior to the purchase price of the Bonds being paid in full by the Subscriber, Lotus Technology may at its option repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a repurchase price equal to the aggregate principal amount of the Bond paid by the Subscriber to the repurchase date (if any).

This Bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement, dated as of [●], 2024 between Lotus Technology Inc., an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands (the “Issuer”) with its principal place of business at No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales (the “Bondholder”). Solely between the Issuer and the Bondholder, the terms of the Bond include those stated in the Subscription Agreement. The Bond is subject to all such terms, and holders are referred to the Subscription Agreement for a statement of all such terms. To the extent permitted by applicable law, in the event of any inconsistency between the terms of this Bond and the terms of the Subscription Agreement, the terms of the Subscription Agreement will control. For the avoidance of doubt, the Agents will not be bound by or have notice of any of the terms and provisions of the Subscription Agreement.

question: Lotus Technology在9月和10月的交付量分别是多少？
rewritten: What were the delivery volumes for Lotus Technology in September and October?
answer:
在2024年9月和10月，Lotus Technology的交付数量如下：在2024年的前九个月，Lotus Technology交付了7,617辆汽车，同比增
长134%。在2024年的前十个月中，Lotus Technology交付了8,631辆汽车。这些数据反映了Lotus Technology在2024年的强劲增
长势头。以上信息基于2024年11月21日之前的可用知识。
content_list: Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered 7,617 vehicles, which marks a 136% increase from the 3,221 units delivered during the same period in 2023. Revenue reached $653 million, representing a 105% year-on-year increase compared to $318 million in 2023. The cost of revenues also rose significantly by 109%, totaling $594 million compared to $284 million the previous year. As a result, gross profit was $59 million, up 75% from $34 million in 2023. However, the gross profit margin decreased to 9% from 11% in the previous year. For the third quarter of 2024, 2,744 vehicles were delivered, a 54% increase from 1,782 in the same quarter of 2023. Revenue in Q3 2024 was $255 million, a 36% increase over $188 million in Q3 2023. The cost of revenues for this quarter was $247 million, up 54% compared to $160 million in Q3 2023.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

Launched: Lotus unveiled its "Hyper Hybrid EV technology" at the Guangzhou Auto Show. The 900V Hyper Hybrid EV technology features a Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging technology: Ultra-Fast Plug-In Charging and Ultra-Fast On-The-Drive Charging, both with industry-leading charging speeds. The Hyper Hybrid technology enables high performance, high efficiency, and a flexible longer driving distance of over 1,100 kilometers.

question: 介绍一下吉利控股？吉利控股给路特斯科技带来什么助力？
rewritten: What is Geely Holding?How does Geely Holding support Lotus Technology?
answer:
Geely Holding 是一家全球领先的移动技术集团，成功孵化和振兴了多个世界知名汽车品牌，包括沃尔沃、极星、领克和极氪。作为唯一拥有跑车基因的吉利控股集团
品牌，Lotus Technology 获得了吉利控股集团在制造、供应链、研发、物流基础设施和人力资源方面的全面支持，正在从英国跑车公司转型为全球高性能电动汽车
的先驱，旨在弥合传统跑车与新一代电动汽车之间的差距。根据 Oliver Wyman 的预测，全球豪华电池电动汽车（BEV）市场（定义为制造商建议零售价超过 80
,000 美元的 BEV）预计将在 2021 年至 2031 年间以 35% 的复合年增长率快速增长，到 2031 年市场将达到近 190 万辆。Geely H
olding 通过技术许可、生产合作和供应链框架等协议支持 Lotus Tech，使 Lotus Tech 能够利用其丰富的资源。具体来说，Geely Hold
ing 负责原材料的订购和检验、生产计划、生产质量控制、制造车辆的物流和运输，以及制造工厂的建设和运营。此外，Geely Holding 还负责获取制造车辆所需
的证书。通过与 Geely Holding 的合作，Lotus Tech 能够快速扩大商业化规模，同时保持轻资产运营模式，减少前期资本投入，控制供应链相关风险，
加速产品开发，并在全球市场上更快速、高效和成本效益地扩展业务。
content_list: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. According to Oliver Wyman, the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% over 2021-2031 and reach a market size of nearly 1.9 million units by 2031.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, or EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds.

Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

Leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain, Lotus Group can quickly establish and maintain relationships with top-tier global suppliers to secure stable supplies of critical components, particularly components such as batteries and automotive chips, which are susceptible to price volatility and supply disruption. Lotus Group believes the strong support from Geely Holding significantly enhances its ability to expand global operations more quickly, efficiently, and cost-effectively than other original equipment manufacturers. Focus on sustainability targeting a fully electric product portfolio. Being at the forefront of electrification and decarbonization, Lotus Group leads the electrification transformation of the luxury car segment and adheres to the highest international ESG standards with an ESG rating of A- by SynTao Green Finance, which awarded A- and above ranking to only 7% of listed companies in its radar. All future new models of Lotus Group will be fully electric, and the Eletre marks Lotus Group's transition to a full line-up of battery electric vehicles (BEVs). In addition, Geely Holding has established a photovoltaic power generation system for its Lotus Global Smart Factory with a capacity of over 16 million kWh in 2023.

Lotus Technology views the manufacturers and suppliers it collaborates with as key partners throughout the vehicle development process. Lotus Technology aims to leverage its partners’ industry expertise to ensure that each vehicle produced meets the company's strict quality standards. Lotus Technology's Collaboration with Geely Holding Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand.

By maintaining a strong focus on research and development and innovation, Lotus Group expects to enjoy significant competitive advantages over other automotive brands in vehicle performance and driving experience. Asset-light business model supported by Geely Holding ecosystem. Lotus Group adopts an asset-light business model that leverages Geely Holding’s extensive resources in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital. Lotus Group benefits from Geely Holding’s newly constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated to electric vehicles in Wuhan, China, to manufacture its battery electric vehicle models, which allows the company to operate under a highly scalable model that can efficiently execute its business plan with limited upfront capital commitment. Leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain, Lotus Group can quickly establish and maintain relationships with top-tier global suppliers to secure stable supplies of critical components, particularly components such as batteries and automotive chips, which are susceptible to price volatility and supply disruption. Lotus Group believes the strong support from Geely Holding significantly enhances its ability to expand global operations more quickly, efficiently, and cost-effectively than other original equipment manufacturers. Focus on sustainability.

Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles. Manufacturing Facility Lotus Technology collaborates with a battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. This manufacturing plant is purpose-built for electric vehicles, featuring advanced manufacturing technologies and covering an area of over one million square meters. The facility has been constructed as a brand-new, world-class plant to produce electric vehicles. It has the capability of conducting stamping, welding, painting, and assembly, and is equipped with testing tracks, a quality inspection center, and a utility power and sewage treatment center. The plant features an advanced system whereby vehicles can be transported into workshops using intelligent driving technology without any human intervention. Additionally, the facility includes an approximately three-kilometer-long track for quality inspection.

Customers can experience driving activities such as racing, drifting, and off-roading on the track, which has nine left turns and seven right turns and can accommodate vehicles driving at speeds of up to 230 kilometers per hour in a straight line. Lotus Technology seeks to partner with reputable suppliers. Lotus Technology leverages the Geely Holding ecosystem for the order of basic auto parts. Lotus Technology intends to use most of the same core suppliers for its vehicles. Lotus Technology has developed close relationships with key suppliers. These include NVIDIA, a global leader in AI computing, which provides its chips for the advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) used in Lotus Technology's vehicles; Qualcomm, a leading developer of semiconductor technologies, which provides 8155 smart cockpit chips; and CATL, a leading manufacturer of lithium-ion batteries, which supplies batteries for Lotus Technology's battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Most of these suppliers are key partners in the Geely Holding ecosystem and have years of strong partnership with Geely Holding.

The results of operations depend significantly on Lotus Technology's ability to achieve vehicle delivery targets, which impacts vehicle sales revenue. It is critical for Lotus Technology to successfully manage production ramp-up and quality control, in cooperation with Geely Holding, to deliver vehicles to customers in targeted volume and of high quality. Currently, Lotus Technology works with a battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. The manufacturing plant has an annual production capacity of 150,000 vehicles.

Lotus Technology manufactures all BEV lifestyle models through a contract manufacturing partnership with Geely Holding, utilizing Geely Holding’s newly constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated to EVs in Wuhan, China, with a planned production capacity of 150,000 units annually. Leveraging Geely Holding’s readily available production capacity, Lotus Technology believes it can execute its business plan with higher scalability and flexibility while limiting its upfront capital commitments compared to most other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Additionally, leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain network, strong bargaining power in procurement, and stable relationships established with reputable suppliers such as NVIDIA, Qualcomm, CATL, and Momenta, Lotus Technology can secure high-quality components at more competitive prices, which it believes would allow for better management of any supply chain disruption risk more effectively compared to other OEMs. Lotus Technology brings customers a luxury retailing experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model to establish and develop direct relationships with customers, covering the entire spectrum of customer experience, both physically and virtually. Lotus Technology operates premium stores in high-footfall locations, providing personalized and exclusive services to create a luxurious purchasing experience for customers.

Lotus Technology believes that its close relationship with Geely Holding provides the company with a unique competitive advantage in its ability to rapidly scale commercialization while maintaining an asset-light operating model with less upfront capital expenditure commitment than other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, including agreements related to technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply of framework, among others, with Geely Holding. The strategic partnership with Geely Holding allows Lotus Technology to effectively control supply chain-related risks and accelerate product development. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

路特斯科技与吉利汽车的关系：（1）吉利汽车（Geely Auto）和路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）属于同一实际控制人。吉利汽车（Geely Auto）约75%的股份由李书福最终实际控制。而路特斯科技的单一最大股东是Lotus AdvancedTechnology Limited Partnership，持有路特斯科技27.6%，也由李书福实际控制。（2）此外， Lotus Technology 与 Geely Holding 签署了多项协议，包括技术许可、制造 合作和供应框架等，以确保供应链相关⻛险得到有效控制，并加速产品开发。这些合作使 Lotus Technology 能够利⽤Geely Holding 的制造能⼒、采购和供应链的议价能⼒、资本投资和运营⽀ 持，从⽽加快其⻋辆的市场投放速度。

Lotus Technology seeks to partner with reputable suppliers. Lotus Technology leverages the Geely Holding ecosystem for the order of basic auto parts. Lotus Technology intends to use most of the same core suppliers for its vehicles. Lotus Technology has developed close relationships with key suppliers. These include NVIDIA, a global leader in AI computing, which provides its chips for the advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) used in Lotus Technology's vehicles; Qualcomm, a leading developer of semiconductor technologies, which provides 8155 smart cockpit chips; and CATL, a leading manufacturer of lithium-ion batteries, which supplies batteries for Lotus Technology's battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Most of these suppliers are key partners in the Geely Holding ecosystem and have years of strong partnership with Geely Holding. Lotus Technology follows its internal process to select suppliers, taking into account quality, cost, and timing. Lotus Technology has a part quality management team responsible for managing and ensuring that suppliers meet quality standards. The method for selecting suppliers depends on the nature of the supplies needed. For general parts that are widely available, Lotus Technology examines proposals from multiple suppliers and chooses based on quality and price competitiveness, among other factors.

Lotus Technology believes that its R&D capability is one of its key competitive strengths. Drawn from Lotus brand sports car design heritage, deep automotive expertise, and next-generation technologies, Lotus Technology's proprietary 800-volt EPA is a high-performance platform for luxury electric vehicles, which was developed over five years of R&D efforts. The platform features supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Such superior design enables Lotus Technology to quickly roll out new models and ramp up production with competitive performance attributes and achieve economies of scale. The global sales digital platform provides a full suite of luxury retailing experiences, including a virtual showroom of the Lotus brand and products, an inquiry, order, purchasing and customization platform, and a reservation system for test driving, product delivery, and aftersales services, among others.

Lotus Technology collaborates with a battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. This manufacturing plant is purpose-built for electric vehicles and incorporates advanced manufacturing technologies. Covering an area of over one million square meters, the plant has the capacity to produce up to 150,000 vehicles per year. The facility has been constructed as a brand-new, world-class plant for the production of electric vehicles. The plant is equipped to conduct stamping, welding, painting, and assembly, and features testing tracks, a quality inspection center, and a utility power and sewage treatment center. It includes an advanced system that allows vehicles to be transported into workshops using intelligent driving technology without any human intervention. The facility is also equipped with an approximately three-kilometer-long track for quality inspection. Customers can experience various driving activities such as racing, drifting, and off-roading on the track. The track includes nine left turns and seven right turns and can accommodate vehicles driving at speeds of up to 230 kilometers per hour in a straight line.

The operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, utilizes technology to create real-time 3D content for passengers.
